body_txt: Related to Remittance Man , but a Remittance Man is not necessarily being hunted. Someone with a Mysterious Past is likely to have this as his backstory as well. Contrast Moses in the Bulrushes , who was too young to know of his heritage. Compare Hidden Backup Prince , who is hidden as a child and doesn't know their true heritage. Also see King Incognito and Defector from Decadence . A Princess in Rags may sometimes be one of these. Heroic Russian Émigré is a subtrope relatively common in 20th-century Western fiction, especially Cold War-era novels: a Russian citizen (often a nobleman) who escaped the Bolsheviks and becomes the West's ally in fighting the Soviet regime.
